success_surface: Structural sheet modification with conditional row insertion across specified range
effective_behaviors: Two-phase approach: discover target rows first, then execute modifications
Descending-order processing when inserting/deleting rows to maintain index stability
Explicit range boundary enforcement during iteration
Tool selection aligned with operation type (openpyxl for structural changes)
Post-modification verification of expected outcome metrics
evidence: Agent scanned entire target range A7:A1000 and identified all matching rows: [18, 32, 43] before any insertion
Agent sorted row indices in descending order: x_rows.sort(reverse=True) before insertion loop
Agent respected instruction-specified range limit: range(7, min(ws.max_row + 1, 1001))
Agent verified row count change: from 2483 to 2486 (confirmed 3 insertions)
Agent used openpyxl.load_workbook() instead of pandas.read_excel() for structural manipulation

memory_items.title: Bottom-Up Row Modification Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When inserting or deleting rows in a worksheet, always process from the last matching position upward.
memory_items.generalization: Downward operations shift all subsequent row indices; reversing the iteration order preserves target accuracy without recalculating positions.

memory_items.title: Discovery-Before-Modification Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Identify and store all target cell/row references before performing any structural changes to the worksheet.
memory_items.generalization: Separating the scanning phase from the modification phase ensures complete coverage even when operations alter document state.

memory_items.title: Instruction Range Enforcement
memory_items.lesson: Apply explicit bounds checking when iterating over rows/columns based on task specifications.
memory_items.generalization: Always respect the requested scope to avoid unintended side effects beyond the intended transformation area.

memory_items.title: Structural Change Tool Selection
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl rather than pandas for operations involving row/column insertion, deletion, or merging.
memory_items.generalization: pandas treats sheets as static data tables; openpyxl preserves Excel structure including formulas, styles, and enables structural manipulations.

memory_items.title: Modification Verification Checkpoint
memory_items.lesson: After completing structural changes, verify measurable outcomes like row count deltas match expectations.
memory_items.generalization: Quantitative validation confirms the operation achieved its intended effect before saving the final output.

edits.content:
## Structural Manipulation Patterns

### Row/Column Insertion Safety
- **Bottom-Up Processing:** When inserting rows, iterate through target positions in descending order (last to first). Inserting from top to bottom shifts subsequent row indices, causing missed targets or incorrect placements.
- **Pre-Scope Discovery:** Identify all target rows/columns before starting modifications. Store references first, then apply changes using the stored list.

### Boundary Control
- Always enforce instruction-specified row/column ranges with explicit checks (min/max guards) to prevent unintended modifications outside the target area.

### Tool Alignment
- Choose openpyxl for structural operations (insert/delete rows/columns, merge cells, formula preservation). Use pandas primarily for value transformations where structure remains unchanged.

### Verification Protocol
- After structural changes, validate by comparing pre/post metrics (row counts, column widths, merged cell regions). Report confirmation before finalizing output.
